data.title: Global One Real Estate Investment Profit Jumps 35% in Six Months Ended March
data.description:
Global One Real Estate Investment's net income attributable to unitholders rose 35% to 3.81 billion yen for the six months ended March, from 2.82 billion yen a year ago.

The REIT's net income per unit rose to 3,802 yen from 2,774 yen a year ago, according to a Tokyo bourse filing on May 21.

Operating revenue jumped 15% to 7.48 billion yen from 6.489 billion yen a year earlier.

It declared a final dividend of 3,835 yen per unit for the six-month period ended March 2025, payable from June 13.

For the six-month period ending September, the company expects net income of 4.53 billion yen, net income per unit of 4,533 yen, and operating revenue of 8.03 billion yen.

For the six-month period ending March 2026, it expects net income of 3,759 million yen, net income per unit of 3,764 yen, and operating revenue of 7,130 million yen.

The company expects to pay dividends of 4,126 yen per unit for the period ending September 2025 and 3,389 yen per unit for the period ending March 2026.

data.title: Cosco Capital Repurchases More Shares
data.description:
Cosco Capital bought back 200,000 shares for about 1.2 million Philippine pesos apiece on Wednesday, according to a Thursday Philippine bourse disclosure.

The retail holding company has so far spent roughly 2.12 billion pesos out of the 4 billion pesos allocated under its buyback program.

data.title: City Cement Cancels Dividend Payout for H2 2024
data.description:
City Cement Co. (SASE:3003) said Thursday its board will no longer pay a cash dividend for the second half of 2024.

The Saudi Arabia-listed cement products manufacturer canceled the payout to maintain its "strong" financial position and until its acquisition is finalized.

data.title: Malaysian Shares Post Six-Day Rout Amid Continued Selling
data.description:
Malaysian shares fell for a sixth straight session on Wednesday amid continued selling pressure.

The FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI, the main gauge of Malaysian stocks, shed 17.78 points, to end 1.15% lower at 1,527.02. The day range was between 1,527.02 and 1,541.46.

In economic news, Malaysia's consumer prices rose 1.4% annually in April, unchanged from the month prior, the Department of Statistics Malaysia said. Core inflation, which excludes volatile items, rose 2% year over year in April, up from 1.9% in the month prior, data showed.

In corporate news, shares of Central Global slid marginally after its unit Central Global Development signed three deals for the proposed acquisition of properties for nearly 3.7 million ringgit. The properties, located in Selangor Darul Ehsan, Malaysia, will be purchased from Chew Hian Tat.

Shares of S P Setia gained about 1% after closing after its profit attributable to owners dropped to 67 million ringgit in the first quarter from 77.3 million ringgit a year earlier.

Morever, Property and construction firm Jiankun International completed a private placement after the listing and quotation of 18.4 million shares on the main market of Bursa Securities.

data.title: Twenty-one people missing as heavy rains soak southern China, triggering landslides
data.description:
Twenty-one people have been trapped after torrential rain triggered landslides in China's southwestern Guizhou province on Thursday, with the military deployed to assist in rescue efforts.

One landslide struck Qingyang village trapping 19 people from eight different households, state broadcaster CCTV reported citing preliminary reports.

A further two were trapped in nearby Changshi township, the official Xinhua news agency reported.

Authorities also issued risk warnings for geological disasters in nearby areas.

China is facing hotter and longer heatwaves and more frequent and unpredictable heavy rain as a result of climate change. The country is especially vulnerable to the effects of climate change, authorities have said, because of its huge population.

Authorities initiated its third highest emergency response for heavy rain in mountainous Guizhou as well as the nearby provinces of Hunan and Jiangxi. More than 400 emergency staff including military officers and firefighters were sent to assist in the rescue mission.

Heavy rains over the last week in China's southern Guangdong province and Guangxi region have killed seven people and left several missing with authorities issuing warnings for severe rain, mountain flooding and geological disasters in the south of the country.

Chinese meteorological data shows 2024 was the warmest year for the country since comparable records began more than six decades ago, the second straight year in which milestones were broken.

data.title: EasyJet Logs Deeper Loss Amid Higher Costs, But Expects Earnings Growth in Fiscal Year — Update
data.description:
By Pierre Bertrand

EasyJet posted a wider pretax loss in the first half of its fiscal year as the company faced higher costs, but said it expected earnings to grow amid strong travel demand in the summer.

The London-listed low-cost airline said bookings made it possible to hit 703 million pounds ($943.4 million) in headline pretax profit so far for the fiscal year to the end of September, compared with the 610 million pounds it reported the previous fiscal year.

Summer months tend to be a lucrative period for low-cost carriers as travelers scramble for cheap deals. EasyJet said bookings for its fiscal third quarter were 80% sold and marginally ahead of their prior-year level. Meanwhile, bookings for the fourth quarter are 42% sold and are also ahead of their prior-year level.

"We remain focused on delivering another record summer this year," Chief Executive Kenton Jarvis said.

The company reported a 401 million pound pretax loss for the six months to the end of March, compared with a 347 million pound loss a year prior, on revenue that grew 8% to 3.53 billion pounds.

The result compares with analysts' forecasts of 399 million pounds in pretax loss and 3.62 billion pounds in revenue, according to Visible Alpha consensus. EasyJet shares traded down 3.6% at 544.2 pence in near mid-day European trading.

Passenger and ancillary revenue in the reporting period rose 5% and 7%, respectively, as easyJet flew 39.5 million passengers, an 8% on-year increase.

EasyJet said the second quarter that ended in March suffered from the timing of Easter, which fell in April this year, when bookings were stronger.

Despite the rise in revenue, headline costs in the reporting period including fuel also rose, up 9% on year to 3.93 billion pounds, the company said.

For the second half of the fiscal year, costs excluding fuel are expected to be slightly higher than a year prior, but the company expects an 8% reduction in fuel costs.

EasyJet's headline pretax loss for the period--a key metric that strips out exceptional and other one-off items--came to 394 million pounds. The company said that result came in line with its internally-compiled consensus.

Write to Pierre Bertrand at pierre.bertrand@wsj.com

Notice of Annual General Meeting

Genflow Biosciences Plc (OTCQB:GENFF) ("Genflow" or "the Company") an emerging leader in the field of longevity research, focused on developing therapeutic solutions for the prevention of age-related diseases is pleased to announce that it will hold its Annual General Meeting ("AGM") at 9am on Thursday 12 June 2025 at One Heddon Street, London, W1B 4BD.

The Notice of the AGM ("Notice") and 2025 Form of Proxy will shortly be available on the Company's website, www.genflowbio.com, and was, where applicable, posted to shareholders on 19 May 2025. Further details of the arrangements for this year's AGM are set out in the Notice.

As announced on 30 April 2025, the Company's Annual Report and Accounts for the year ended 31 December 2024 are also available on the website and has, where applicable, also been posted to registered shareholders.

About Genflow Biosciences

Founded in 2020, Genflow Biosciences Plc. (OTCQB:GENFF), a biotechnology company headquartered in the UK with R&D facilities in Belgium, is pioneering gene therapies to decelerate the aging process, with the goal of promoting longer and healthier lives while mitigating the financial, emotional, and social impacts of a fast-growing aging global population. Genflow's lead compound, GF-1002, works through the delivery of a centenarian variant of the SIRT6 gene which has yielded promising preclinical results. Scheduled to begin in 2025, Genflow's clinical trial aims to explore the potential benefits of GF-1002 in treating MASH (Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Steatohepatitis), the most prevalent chronic liver disease for which there is no effective treatments. data.title: French Yields Rise Amid US Fiscal Worries
data.description:
France’s 10-year OAT yield held above 3.3%, the highest level since May 14, amid growing investor unease over the worsening US fiscal outlook ahead of a House vote on President Donald Trump’s proposed tax-cut bill.

Adding to the pressure, a weak $16 billion auction of 20-year US Treasury bonds on Wednesday followed closely on the heels of Moody’s downgrade of the US credit rating.

In Europe, markets assessed a mix of economic signals.

Germany’s Ifo Business Climate Index rose to 87.5 in May, the highest reading since June 2024 and slightly above forecasts, suggesting a modest improvement in business sentiment.

Meanwhile, PMI data revealed continued weakness in France’s private sector, as both manufacturing and services posted further declines.

data.title: Swatch Group Would Benefit from a Governance Overhaul — Market Talk
data.description: A transformational change in Swatch Group's governance would be a step in the right direction, Bernstein analysts write in a note. The Swiss watchmaker is grappling with waning demand for high-end goods, which is particularly acute in China, a key market for both the company and the luxury industry. "Swatch Group has high potential...but is chronically under-managed," Bernstein says. However, one opportunity was lost when the attempt to elect activist investor Steven Wood to the group's board of directors failed, they say. According to the company, the vast majority of shareholders voted against Wood's election. Shares are down 0.4% at 147.60 Swiss francs. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

data.title: Shalfa Facilities Management Wins Security Guard Services Deal from Saudi Authority
data.description:
Shalfa Facilities Management (SASE:9613) secured a 12-month contract to provide security guard services for facilities affiliated with the Zakat, Tax and Customs Authority.

The services will be provided for the authority's main headquarters, residential cities, and administrative offices at the ports and branches across Saudi Arabia, according to a Thursday release.

The facility management services company will secure a 50% share of the 35.4 million-Saudi-riyal contract after all obligations and financial dues are settled to the workforce, suppliers, and government entities involved.

data.title: SocGen's Overnight Economic News Summary
data.description:
Societe Generale in its early Thursday economic news summary pointed out:

— US dollar mixed, slump in oil prices spurs relief in bonds. OPEC+ considering 411,000 supply increase in July. United States deficit concerns, "sloppy" 20-year U.S. Treasury auction (tail 1.1bps) lifted 10-year UST to 4.61% and 30-year to 5.10%. G7: U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessent and Japan Finance Minister Kato reaffirm foreign exchange to be market-determined, levels not discussed. South Korea's ministry of finance says foreign exchange talks with the U.S. are ongoing.

#— Germany's manufacturing PMI up to 48.8 in May from 48.4 in April, services drop to 47.2 from 49.0. France's manufacturing improves to 49.5 from 48.7, services +0.1pt to 47.4. The United Kingom manufacturing dips to 45.1 from 45.4, services up to 50.2 from 49.0.

— Japanese investors bought 2,825 billion yen in international bonds last week, the most since September 2023. This is on top of 1,929 billion yen international bond buys made the week before, marking the largest two weeks of international bond purchases on record.

— Day ahead: U.S. weekly jobless claims. U.S. and U.K. PMIs. Federal Reserve speaker Williams. German Ifo. European Central Bank's Vujcic, Nagel, Elderson, Guindos, Escriva. ECB accounts of April meeting. Bank of England's Breeden, Dhingra and Pill.

— Nikkei -0.8%, EUR 10-year IRS unchanged at 2.60%, Brent crude -1.8% at $63.8/barrel, Gold +0.8% at $3,330/oz.

data.title: German Yields Steady as US Fiscal Woes Deepen
data.description:
Germany’s 10-year Bund yield held steady at 2.64%, while the 30-year yield climbed to a two-month high of 3.18%, as investor concerns over the deteriorating US fiscal outlook intensified ahead of a House of Representatives vote on President Donald Trump’s tax-cut bill.

Additional pressure on bond markets stemmed from weak demand at Wednesday’s $16 billion auction of 20-year US Treasury bonds, following Moody’s recent downgrade of the U.S. credit rating.

In Europe, markets digested a series of economic reports.

Germany’s Ifo Business Climate Index rose to 87.5 in May, its highest level since June 2024 and slightly above expectations of 87.4, indicating that uncertainty among businesses has eased somewhat.

However, PMI data showed an unexpected renewed contraction in Germany’s private sector, driven by simultaneous declines in both manufacturing and services activity.

data.title: Duluth Holdings Inc. to Report First Quarter 2025 Financial Results on June 5
data.description:
MOUNT HOREB, Wis., May 22, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Duluth Holdings Inc. (dba, Duluth Trading Company) (“Duluth Trading”) , a lifestyle brand of men’s and women’s casual wear, workwear and accessories, today announced that it will report first quarter 2025 financial results before market on Thursday, June 5, 2025.

A conference call and audio webcast with analysts and investors will be held on Thursday, June 5, 2025 at 9:30 am Eastern Time, to discuss the results and answer questions.

Live conference call: 1-844-875-6915 (domestic) or 1-412-317-6711 (international)

Conference call replay available through June 12, 2025: 1-877-344-7529 (domestic) or 1-412-317-0088 (international)

  • Replay access code: 8123705
  • Live and archived webcast: ir.duluthtrading.com
  • To expedite entry into the call and avoid waiting for a live operator, investors may pre-register at https://dpregister.com/sreg/10199243/ff038e2354 and enter their contact information. Investors will then be issued a personalized phone number and pin to dial into the live conference call.

About Duluth Trading

Duluth Trading is a growing lifestyle brand for the Modern, Self-Reliant American. Based in Mount Horeb, Wisconsin, we offer high quality, solution-based casual wear, workwear and accessories for men and women who lead a hands-on lifestyle and who value a job well-done. We provide our customers an engaging and entertaining experience. Our marketing incorporates humor and storytelling that conveys the uniqueness of our products in a distinctive, fun way, and our products are sold exclusively through our content-rich website, catalogs, and “store like no other” retail locations. We are committed to outstanding customer service backed by our “No Bull Guarantee” - if it’s not right, we’ll fix it. Visit our website at http://www.duluthtrading.com/

data.title: 104 Buys CPC Corp.'s Bonds Worth NT$100 Million
data.description:
104 Corp. acquired 100 units of CPC Corp.'s unsecured corporate bond-A on Thursday for NT$100 million.

The human resources consulting services company purchased the units at NT$1 million each, according to a same-day Taiwan Exchange filing.

This long-term investment accounts for 8.66% of total assets and 18.41% of equity attributable to owners, it added.

data.title: Strategic Minerals Plans Diamond Drilling Campaign for Redmoor Critical Minerals Project
data.description:
Strategic Minerals said Thursday its subsidiary Cornwall Resources is planning a new diamond drilling campaign in late June, comprising nine boreholes totaling 5,300 meters at the Redmoor tungsten-tin-copper project in Cornwall, UK.

The mineral exploration and production company's six-month program will focus on the Redmoor resource and exploration target. Data gathered from the drill holes will support a new JORC-compliant Mineral Resource Estimate for Redmoor, which is expected in the first quarter of 2026.
